steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's core assumption
steps.content: The student inferred same egg-laying depth from identical pre-hatching temperatures (20–22°C). This reasoning assumes that identical temperatures in different regions correspond to identical depths.

steps.title: Test which finding breaks that assumption
steps.content:
Choice C breaks this assumption by introducing regional temperature variation: if the same depth has different temperatures in the two regions, then identical temperatures across regions point to different depths. The others don't target this logic: A supports the hypothesis, B focuses on post-hatching mobility, and D addresses suitability.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
